era_context: The Middle Bronze Age in the Eastern Mediterranean and Near East. The Old Babylonian period under Samsu-iluna; Egypt in the Second Intermediate Period; the Hittite Old Kingdom consolidating power; Mycenaean Greece in Early Bronze Age III; the Indus Valley Civilization at its height.

events.title: Samsu-iluna's military campaigns in Mesopotamia
events.description: During his reign, Samsu-iluna conducted military campaigns to maintain Babylonian control over Mesopotamian territories. These campaigns were directed against the Kassites to the east and other rival powers. The campaigns were partially successful but ultimately could not prevent the gradual erosion of Babylonian hegemony. The year -1756 falls within a period of active military engagement, though specific campaigns for this year are not individually documented.

sources.name: Cuneiform administrative texts and later king lists

events.certainty_note: Samsu-iluna's military activities are attested, but specific campaigns are not dated to individual years in surviving sources. This entry reflects the general military context of his reign.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are well-documented for -1756. The broader narrative of Babylonian decline during Samsu-iluna's reign is accepted, but the year-by-year progression of events is not preserved in surviving sources. Any claim of specific significance for this year would be speculative.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -1756 is extremely sparse. Cuneiform sources provide administrative and legal texts but rarely date events to specific years in a way that modern scholars can verify. The chronology of the Old Babylonian period remains debated, with some scholars placing events 50–150 years later than the conventional chronology. For this year, we are largely dependent on inference from broader patterns rather than specific documentation.
